task_completion.description: Apply 'Heading 1' style to the chapter title 'Chapter 4: The Crossing'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Chapter 4: The Crossing' is visually formatted as a heading (larger, bold font) and/or the style selector in the toolbar shows 'Heading 1' when the cursor is on it.

task_completion.description: Add page numbers to the footer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Page numbers (e.g., '1', '2') are visible in the footer area at the bottom of the document pages.

task_completion.description: Mark terms from the JSON list as index entries
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terms in the document text (e.g., 'Jeremiah Holloway', 'Oregon Trail') have a gray background indicating an index mark, or the 'Insert Index Entry' dialog is visible being used on these terms.

task_completion.description: Insert a page break at the end of the document and add the title 'Index' (Heading 1 style)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new page at the end of the document starts with the title 'Index' formatted as a Heading 1.

An Alphabetical Index is visible on the last page under the 'Index' title, automatically listing the marked terms (e.g., 'Chimney Rock', 'Cholera') along with their page numbers in a structured block.

task_completion.description: Save the finalized document as Holloway_Chapter4_Indexed.odt
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word processor's title bar displays 'Holloway_Chapter4_Indexed.odt' or a 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved with this exact name in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a word processor GUI to format the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a word processor (like LibreOffice Writer) open with the document, rather than terminal commands or a plain text editor modifying the ODT file.

integrity.description: The Alphabetical Index was generated using the word processor's built-in feature
integrity.visual_evidence: The Alphabetical Index appears as a generated field (often with a light gray background in LibreOffice) and the agent is seen using the 'Insert Index or Table of Contents' dialog, rather than manually typing the index.

integrity.description: The agent actually marked the terms in the text
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the body text to insert index entries (using the 'Insert Index Entry' dialog or similar), rather than just typing an index at the end without marking the text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 10 terms to be indexed are: Jeremiah Holloway, Martha Holloway, Cumberland Gap, Oxen, Fort Laramie, Independence Rock, Chimney Rock, Oregon Trail, Cholera, and Wagon. The agent must mark at least 8 of these 10 terms as index entries. The final file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/Holloway_Chapter4_Indexed.odt.
